corridors.primaryReadinessConstraint: Missing extracted directional inventory is the primary readiness constraint. Secondary constraints are unscored confidence, unavailable review-bucket exposure, and unvalidated corridor-level containment for segment records.
corridors.recommendedRemediationPath: Extract US 90 inventory first, normalize references, score candidates, then run containment and bucket review.
corridors.readinessImpact: NOT READY until corridor inventory, candidates, confidence scoring, review buckets, and corridor-level containment are available.

blockers.description: No extracted corridor inventory exists for this corridor in the V713 evidence set.
blockers.impact: No directional candidate records are available to score, contain, review, or consume.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Create corridor-specific extraction inventory and segment records.

blockers.description: Directional candidate count is 0 because extraction has not produced corridor records.
blockers.impact: Service consumers have no candidate payloads; fail-closed behavior remains correct.
blockers.readinessEffect: Blocks route-intelligence onboarding and incident enrichment.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Generate directional candidates from extracted inventory without changing runtime behavior.

blockers.description: High-, medium-, and review-required candidate availability are all 0.
blockers.impact: No candidate can be promoted or reviewed for directional use.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Run existing confidence scoring after inventory exists; do not modify confidence logic.

blockers.description: County systems pass globally, but these corridor segments cannot pass corridor-level containment because no segment-level county attribution exists.
blockers.impact: No leakage is detected; tests are unavailable for these corridors.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Run corridor containment after extraction for Liberty, Montgomery, and San Jacinto.

blockers.description: Required tags and normalized references cannot be audited because records are absent; missing_ref, missing_oneway, and missing_county exposure is unknown.
blockers.impact: Manual review load cannot be estimated.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Audit ref/oneway/county metadata after extraction.

blockers.description: Review bucket counts are unavailable for all protected buckets until extraction.
blockers.impact: Cannot distinguish expected exclusions from true defects.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Produce bucket counts and keep fail-closed exclusions unchanged.

blockers.description: Directional geometry ambiguity cannot be assessed without extracted segments; risk is higher for TX 146 and FM 1960.
blockers.impact: May increase manual review and segmentation corrections.
blockers.readinessEffect: May delay readiness after inventory exists.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Review geometry continuity, divided-road directionality, and local naming once records exist.

blockers.description: Segment boundaries are not established for these corridors.
blockers.impact: May create partial coverage or duplicate corridor candidates.
blockers.readinessEffect: May delay confidence and containment validation.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Normalize segment cuts before scoring.

blockers.description: US/TX/FM naming variants and aliases have not been normalized for the Phase 1 corridors.
blockers.impact: Reference matching may miss records or split a corridor identity.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Define corridor aliases and canonical corridor IDs during extraction.

blockers.description: Existing validated inventory is concentrated in the I-69 / US 59 prototype corridor.
blockers.impact: Phase 1 corridors inherit no ready inventory from the prototype.
blockers.readinessEffect: Blocks expansion until onboarding moves beyond prototype evidence.
blockers.requiredRemediation: Treat as intentional prototype scope, then perform corridor-first onboarding.

corridors.primaryReadinessConstraint: Missing extracted directional inventory is the primary readiness constraint, with elevated anticipated ambiguity from state-highway shared alignments and boundary interactions.
corridors.recommendedRemediationPath: Extract TX 146 inventory, resolve shared-alignment/reference ambiguity, then score candidates and run containment before any promotion review.
corridors.readinessImpact: NOT READY until corridor inventory, candidates, confidence scoring, review buckets, and corridor-level containment are available.

containmentAssessment.note: Global county containment remains passing; corridor-level containment is blocked only by absent corridor segment inventory.

supportingEvidence: All three corridors share confirmed inventory and candidate-generation blockers.
Confidence and review-bucket blockers are understood as direct consequences of absent extracted records.
County containment systems remain passing and do not independently cause failure.
Prototype-only scope explains why evidence exists for I-69 / US 59 but not these Phase 1 corridors.
